questions.question: What does list virtualization fundamentally do to improve performance?
options: It compresses list items to reduce memory footprint
It renders only items visible in the viewport instead of all items
It paginates the list automatically to limit DOM nodes to 100
It uses Web Workers to render list items off the main thread

questions.question: What CSS requirement must the container have when using vue-virtual-scroller's RecycleScroller?
options: overflow: hidden to prevent scroll events from bubbling
display: grid for proper item positioning
A fixed height for the container element
position: relative for absolute item positioning

questions.question: When should you NOT use list virtualization?
options: For SEO-critical content that needs to be in the initial HTML
When the list has pagination controls
When the list uses v-for with a :key binding
